---
title: "Spatial niche identification"
description: "Learn recurring spatial neighborhoods with NicheCompass."
categories: [openpipeline_spatial]
order: 65
---

[`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial}<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>

A **niche** is a recurring spatial neighborhood, a local mix of cell types that
appears across a tissue. **`openpipeline_spatial`** uses
[NicheCompass](https://nichecompass.readthedocs.io/) to learn niches from a
processed spatial object, on top of curated gene programs (sets of genes that act
together).

## Options

| Workflow / component | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`niche/nichecompass_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/workflows/niche/nichecompass_leiden.qmd) | Identify niches: embed cells niche-aware, then cluster |
| `nichecompass/gene_program_mask` | Prepare the gene-program mask the workflow consumes |

## Example: NicheCompass

For example, on a [spatially-processed](process-spatial.qmd) object (with
coordinates in `.obsm["spatial"]`):

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline_spatial.git \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/niche/nichecompass_leiden/main.nf \
  --input processed_spatial.h5mu \
  --output niches.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Key choices

- **Prerequisite:** an ingested, [processed spatial](process-spatial.qmd) object
  that carries `.obsm["spatial"]`.
- **Gene programs:** `nichecompass/gene_program_mask` builds the mask of
  co-acting gene sets the model reasons over; the workflow embeds cells into a
  niche-aware latent space from it and clusters them.

## Output

A niche-aware embedding and per-cell niche labels written into the object,
alongside the cell-type annotations from earlier steps.
